reports.title: Trump Is Trying to Kill 1,000 State AI Bills. The States Are Not Listening.
reports.subtitle: The White House wants one federal standard for AI. States are passing laws anyway. A Utah Republican who used to work at Google is running for senate on defying his own party's president.

reports.title: Europe's Nuclear Experts Are Watching Witkoff and Kushner Negotiate Iran. They Are Not Calm.
reports.subtitle: European diplomats who spent 12 years building the 2015 nuclear accord have been sidelined from the current talks. They think a bad deal is more likely than no deal, and that a bad deal is worse.

reports.title: SCOTUS Heard the Birthright Citizenship Case. The Justices Are Not Buying It.
reports.subtitle: Across the ideological spectrum, Supreme Court justices pressed Trump's solicitor general on why the 14th Amendment means something different now. He had no good answer.

reports.title: The Republican Party Is Having Its Immigration Fight In Public. One Side Wants Votes. The Other Wants Deportations.
reports.subtitle: Rep. Maria Elvira Salazar's Dignity Act would give pre-2021 undocumented immigrants legal status but no citizenship. A new PAC is spending to end her career. Manufacturers are endorsing her. The House majority is at stake.

reports.title: Abortion Bans Increased Abortions. The Pro-Life Movement Won the Law and Lost the Outcome.
reports.subtitle: The US hit 1.14 million abortions in 2024, more than any year in the previous decade. Medication abortion now accounts for 25% of all procedures. State bans did not stop abortions. They moved them.

reports.title: Oil Companies Celebrated Trump. Now They Are Watching WTI Drop 9% in a Day.
reports.subtitle: Energy Secretary Wright admitted gas might not fall below $3 until 2027. WTI crude dropped 9.6% Sunday. The 'drill baby drill' coalition is discovering that war in the Middle East and Trump's tariff-driven recession fears are worse for energy markets than any Biden regulation.

reports.title: Iran Closes Hormuz Again. The Ceasefire Was Never a Ceasefire.
reports.subtitle: Tehran and Washington are each enforcing their version of the truce while calling the other side's actions a violation.

reports.title: Trump Says Tariffs Will Replace the Income Tax. The Math Doesn't Work. That's Not the Point.
reports.subtitle: The proposal is arithmetically impossible and legally requires Congress. It is doing its intended job anyway.

reports.title: The SAVE Act Is Not About Noncitizens. It Is About Who Controls the Voter List.
reports.subtitle: Trump's push to use DHS's SAVE program for election verification is dividing election officials ahead of midterms, and polls show it is popular.

reports.title: OpenAI's Drug Discovery AI Is Closed-Access. That Is the Whole Strategy.
reports.subtitle: GPT-Rosalind can access 50+ scientific databases but researchers cannot see its reasoning, weights, or error analysis. OpenAI is positioning to own the epistemic layer of pharmaceutical discovery.

reports.title: The FTC Is Doing to NewsGuard What Rhode Island Did to Comic Books in 1956.
reports.subtitle: Courts have repeatedly ruled that government pressure on private intermediaries to suppress speech is unconstitutional. The Trump administration is doing it anyway, from a different direction.

reports.title: Anthropic Built an AI That Can Break Any System. It Is Not Releasing It. That Decision Has Already Expired.
reports.subtitle: Claude Mythos can exploit software vulnerabilities faster than any human security team. Anthropic gave it to 40 hand-picked companies. Everyone else is now waiting to see who weaponizes it first.

reports.title: Bessent Said No. Then Said Yes. The Russia Oil Sanctions Reversal Is Not a Mistake. It Is the Policy.
reports.subtitle: Two days after Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told reporters the Russian oil sanctions waiver would not be renewed, the Treasury renewed it. Ukraine is calling it a betrayal. Russia is calling it cooperation.

reports.title: The DOJ Is Asking Courts to Erase the Jan. 6 Seditious Conspiracy Convictions. That Is Not Forgiveness. It Is Revision.
reports.subtitle: Jeanine Pirro's filing doesn't argue the defendants are innocent. It argues that erasing their convictions is 'in the interests of justice.' The two things are not the same, and the courts are being asked to pretend they are.

reports.title: Trump Signed an Executive Order for Psychedelics. The War on Drugs Is Still Running in the Same Building.
reports.subtitle: Ibogaine remains a Schedule I controlled substance. The executive order Trump signed with Joe Rogan in the room does not change that. What it does is create a path for veterans to get it before it is legal, without the protections that legality would provide.

reports.title: The Fed Is in a Box It Has Not Been in Since 1979. This Time the Shocks Are Compounding.
reports.subtitle: War, tariffs, and an oil supply squeeze are hitting simultaneously. The Federal Reserve has a mandate to fight inflation and support employment. It cannot do both at once. Markets are pretending it can.

reports.title: DeepSeek Is Abandoning Nvidia. Jensen Huang Says That's Catastrophic. He's Right for the Wrong Reasons.
reports.subtitle: The real threat isn't that China gets better chips. It's that America loses the software lock-in that made export controls worth having.

reports.title: The New York Times Got the Supreme Court's Internal Memos. The Leak Matters More Than the Memos.
reports.subtitle: SCOTUS justices are publicly feuding over the shadow docket just as someone with access handed their private deliberations to a reporter.

reports.title: The EPA Just Erased the Legal Foundation for All Federal Climate Rules. The Courts Are the Only Thing Left.
reports.subtitle: Repealing the 2009 Endangerment Finding doesn't just end emissions regulations. It ends the EPA's legal authority to regulate greenhouse gases under any future administration without an act of Congress.

reports.title: The EU's AI Hiring Rules Go Live in 105 Days. Most Companies Have Never Audited Their AI. That's About to Become Very Expensive.
reports.subtitle: August 2 is the deadline for mandatory AI hiring bias audits under the EU AI Act. The auditors don't exist yet and the companies aren't ready.

reports.title: Greek Workers Are Striking Against a Law That Extends the Work Week. The Government Is Calling It Modernization.
reports.subtitle: Greece's conservative government is trying to pass labor reforms that allow private-sector employers to set longer hours. Unions are shutting down the country for the second time this month to stop it.
